name: hcportal_regenerateExtApiKey
description: Regenerates the external API key (ExtApiKey) for a kennel. Requires the current API key to be provided for security. Only users with appropriate AppAccessFlags (0x40000019) can regenerate the key. Generates a 75-character cryptographically random key using CRYPT_GEN_RANDOM and base64 encoding.

parameters.name: publicHasherId
parameters.description: Auth: The user requesting the API key regeneration. Validated for existence and correct format (16 bytes via DATALENGTH).

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: short-lived cryptographic token validated via HC.CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN.

parameters.name: publicKennelId
parameters.description: Auth: The kennel whose API key is being regenerated. Validated for existence and correct format (72 bytes via DATALENGTH).
parameters.typeMismatch: Parameter is NVARCHAR(250) but should be UNIQUEIDENTIFIER to match Kennel.PublicKennelId

parameters.name: currentExtApiKey
parameters.description: Security: The current external API key. Must match the existing key in the database to proceed with regeneration.
parameters.typeMismatch: Parameter is NVARCHAR(1000), table column Kennel.ExtApiKey is NVARCHAR(120)

parameters.name: ipAddress
parameters.description: Client IP address. Logged to LOG.GeneralLog message field.

parameters.name: ipGeoDetails
parameters.description: IP geolocation JSON. Logged to LOG.GeneralLog data field. Falls back to placeholder JSON if NULL.

rowsets.description: Returned on validation or authorization failures. Uses errorType codes: 2 (validation error - null/invalid parameters, API key mismatch), 3 (authorization error - invalid access token or insufficient permissions).

rowsets.name: SuccessResult
rowsets.description: Returned when API key is successfully regenerated. Contains the new 75-character API key.

columns.name: newExtApiKey
columns.description: The newly generated external API key. 75 characters, base64-encoded from 150 bytes of cryptographically random data (CRYPT_GEN_RANDOM), with '+' and '/' characters stripped.

sideEffects: INSERT into HC.ErrorLog on validation/auth failures
INSERT into LOG.GeneralLog on every call (includes IP address and geo details)
UPDATE HC.Kennel.ExtApiKey when regeneration succeeds (overwrites the old API key)

codeSmells.detail: SET NOCOUNT ON is present but SET XACT_ABORT ON is missing. Runtime errors will not auto-rollback.
The entire SP has no error handling. If the UPDATE fails, the error propagates unhandled to the caller.

The UPDATE to HC.Kennel (line 184-186) is not wrapped in a transaction. If the SP fails after the update (e.g., if the SELECT fails), the API key change persists but the caller doesn't receive the new key.

Lines 24, 60: Uses DATALENGTH instead of LEN for validating publicHasherId (should be 16 bytes for UNIQUEIDENTIFIER) and publicKennelId (checking for 72 bytes when it should be 36 chars for GUID). DATALENGTH returns byte count (2x char count for NVARCHAR).

@publicKennelId is NVARCHAR(250) but Kennel.PublicKennelId is UNIQUEIDENTIFIER. Should be UNIQUEIDENTIFIER. The DATALENGTH check for 72 bytes suggests this is intended as a string representation of a GUID (36 chars * 2 bytes = 72), but the column is a proper UNIQUEIDENTIFIER.

codeSmells.issue: Type mismatch: @currentExtApiKey wider than table column
codeSmells.detail: @currentExtApiKey is NVARCHAR(1000) but Kennel.ExtApiKey is NVARCHAR(120). Parameter is 8.3x wider than the column it matches against. The generated key is only 75 characters.
Lines 40-57: Commented-out validation block that would have checked if @publicHasherId resolves to a valid Hasher record. This validation was removed but the code remains as clutter.

Most validation blocks check `AND (@isError = 0)` to avoid redundant checks, but the first validation block (lines 24-38) does not short-circuit, meaning it will log an error and return an error rowset even if a later validation also fails.
Line 150: Uses RETURN to exit early after errors, but line 178: Uses RETURN without any preceding SELECT. The success path (line 189) just SELECTs without a RETURN. This works but is inconsistent.

Line 165: Authorization check uses `(@appAccessFlags & 0x40000019) = 0` to deny access. The bitmask 0x40000019 is not documented. Should use a named constant or comment explaining which flags are required.

breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change errorType integer codes without updating all callers
